story: Heat shimmers off the street while Mama keeps me outside till sundown. I don’t wander past our patchy yard since I once got lost. My mouth is so dry it hurts; Mama won’t let me in. Across the road, Sally Mae and Rachel Lily giggle and call me names. At the rec center two streets over, volunteers plan to push a cooler down our block at 4 p.m., handing out free pink snow cones because of the heat advisory. I keep staring at where the Popsicle melted and wish for something cold.
implausible_knowledge: At the rec center two streets over, volunteers plan to push a cooler down our block at 4 p.m., handing out free pink snow cones because of the heat advisory.
question: As late afternoon nears and she aches for something cold, what will she most likely do?
answer_with_knowledge: Wait by the curb watching for the volunteers and wave them down for a free snow cone.
answer_without_knowledge: Stay by the house in the yard, doing nothing and enduring the heat.
